title: Read a pipeline for secret and trust gaps
description: Inspect .gitlab-ci.yml and variable settings and report exposure, runner-trust, rules, and artifact risks.
body: Use when asked whether a GitLab pipeline is safe to merge. Actions: read the .gitlab-ci.yml and variable settings and verify secret variables are protected and masked, runner tags keep untrusted MR jobs off privileged runners, rules are used without an only/except mix and do not run deploy jobs on untrusted refs, artifacts:paths are scoped with expire_in, and fork MR pipelines hold no protected variables. Do not assume defaults are safe (an unprotected variable reaches every branch). Record each gap with the job/setting and classify as secret-exposure, runner-trust, determinism, or supply-chain. This skill is read-only and must not modify variables or run the pipeline without runtime permission. Validation signals include a concrete list of jobs/settings and their risk class. Risk boundary: report any protected-variable-on-untrusted-ref or untrusted-job-on-privileged-runner as a blocking finding and recommend escalation.
